description_long: This object is a retro-style computer monitor with an integrated keyboard and a large red trackball, reminiscent of early personal computers from the late 20th century. The monitor features a thick, rounded bezel framing a square display, which shows orange text on a black background, evoking classic monochrome CRT screens. The keyboard is built into the base, with raised keys and a prominent red trackball positioned to the right, suggesting a unique input method. The casing is a uniform gray color, with subtle venting details on the sides and back for heat dissipation. The rear of the monitor is boxy and extends backward, typical of CRT technology, and includes a couple of small, dark circular features that may be access panels or cable ports. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements, and the materials appear to be primarily molded plastic with some internal metal components.
description: A retro gray computer monitor with a thick bezel, built-in keyboard, and a large red trackball, featuring a boxy CRT-style rear and orange text display.
description_view_0: Side view showing the monitor's deep, boxy rear and the sloped base with the keyboard and red trackball visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the square display with orange text, thick rounded bezel, full keyboard, and prominent red trackball on the right.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the monitor's depth, venting details, and the sloped keyboard base.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the monitor with venting details and two small dark circular features, with no visible screen or controls.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rounded, and made of matte gray plastic, with no color contrast or decorative elements. It frames the display area with soft corners and a utilitarian appearance.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by an integrated, sloped base that houses the keyboard and trackball, forming a single molded plastic unit with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: A large red trackball is positioned to the right of the keyboard on the base, with no other visible buttons or controls on the monitor itself.
